title: Pyrrosia glabra
taxonomy: Pyrrosia glabra is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants, now more commonly referred to as Polypodiopsida or Polypodiales. However, it's more commonly classified under the family Polypodiaceae but more specifically within the subfamily Polypodioideae. Pyrrosia glabra is a member of the genus Pyrrosia, which includes about 80 species of epiphytic ferns. The genus Pyrrosia is part of the family Polypodiaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of ferns. Pyrrosia glabra is also commonly referred to as the Smooth Pyrrosia.
uses: Pyrrosia glabra is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, where it is believed to have various health benefits. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. intro: Pyrrosia glabra, also known as the smooth rhinophyllum, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The smooth rhinophyllum has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against cell damage, and combat viral infections. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrosia glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
